goal: Learn the AWS cloud fundamentals needed for data science by building an end-to-end workflow that stores data, trains a machine learning model, saves its artifacts, and deploys the trained model in AWS

user_context.background: Data scientist who works locally with Python and notebooks and understands the data science lifecycle, but has no professional cloud-computing experience
unknown: AWS regions, availability zones, and service selection
IAM users, roles, policies, and access permissions
Amazon S3 for datasets and trained-model artifacts
Amazon EC2 and cloud compute fundamentals
Programmatic AWS access from Python with boto3
Amazon SageMaker notebooks and processing workflows
Deploying a trained model to a SageMaker endpoint
Connecting AWS storage, compute, and machine-learning services
Testing predictions from a deployed endpoint
user_context.constraints: I prefer hands-on, project-based instruction focused specifically on AWS for data science. The curriculum should use a real dataset and lead to one understandable end-to-end workflow that includes data storage, model training, artifact management, deployment, and test predictions. Include practical exposure to both the AWS CLI and boto3. Avoid generic certification-preparation courses, multi-cloud comparisons, advanced Kubernetes, enterprise-scale data engineering, Spark clusters, complex CI/CD, and advanced MLOps platforms. Do not prioritize billing, cost optimization, or resource-cleanup instruction.
